claim: World Bank reports South Asian cities generate over 200 million tons of solid waste annually, collection rates below 60 percent, open dumping as primary disposal, and projects waste generation doubling by 2050 without intervention.

source.title: What a Waste 2.0: Solid Waste Management in South Asia

method: Reviewed What a Waste database, South Asia regional data, and waste-management indicators.
limitations: Waste-generation estimates are extrapolated from national averages.
Collection-rate estimates may not capture informal collection.

claim: A 2012 landmark study estimated informal waste pickers handle 15-30 percent of recycling in developing-country cities, achieving higher recycling rates than formal systems, and representing 1-2 percent of the urban population.

source.title: Role of informal sector recycling in waste management in developing countries

method: Reviewed informal-sector estimation methodology, recycling rates, and urban-population proportions.
limitations: Informal-sector data is inherently difficult to verify systematically.
Study data is from early 2010s and may not reflect current conditions.
